headline: Bardella's contradiction of Le Pen on pensions confounds French far right
description: Jordan Bardella, who would stand as the Rassemblement National's presidential candidate if Marine Le Pen's election ban is upheld, has broken with his boss's line on a crucial policy issue.

headline: US renews Iran attacks, Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz
'The egalitarianism of hunter-gatherer societies has often been exaggerated'
United Kingdom faces new night of riots in Belfast following stabbing incident
description: After a Sudanese man stabbed a Northern Irish man, dozens of masked men targeted homes occupied by people of immigrant origin. As in recent similar episodes, far-right activists are exploiting the incident and amplifying it on social media.

headline: Chinese authorities pressure Beijing's Institut français to halt screenings of films featuring same-sex relationships
description: Despite a reciprocal agreement guaranteeing programming freedom within cultural institutes, Chinese police officers personally visited the site to intimidate staff and to ensure the cancelation of the screenings.

headline: Young Chinese red panda couple 'infiltrates' Taiwan
description: A 3-year-old male and a 2-year-old female, both red pandas from Shanghai, have arrived at the Taipei Zoo in exchange for two gibbons. This marks the first such exchange since 2014. Given the current tension across the Taiwan Strait, however, the gift is far from innocent.

headline: 'In liberal democracies, the grace period for leaders rarely lasts more than six months'
description: Financial crises, wars, Covid: Over the past several years, an uninterrupted series of external shocks has eroded citizens' trust in representative democracy, observes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'
description: Although rooted in a bygone era and battered by Donald Trump, the Group of Seven continues to meet purely out of habit, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris, one week before the summit chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron in Evian.

headline: 'Disclosure Day': Steven Spielberg goes back to the future
description: After two more personal, retrospective feature films, the American filmmaker revisits his passion for extraterrestrials.

headline: 'The cultural boycott of Israeli director Nadav Lapid is an intellectual failure'
description: Russian, Israeli and Iranian filmmakers should not be threatened with erasure to atone for crimes committed by governments they often fiercely oppose, argue a group of film figures, including Natalie Portman, Justine Triet and Jacques Audiard, in an open letter.
